_comment: Example Power BI Analysis Tool Configuration with Context Enrichment
_description: This example shows how to configure the Power BI Analysis Tool with Microsoft Copilot-style context enrichment for simplified natural language queries
user_question: What is the number of customers with Complete CGR in the Italian BU in week 1?

business_mappings._comment: Map natural language phrases to DAX filter expressions

field_synonyms._comment: Define alternative names for fields that users might use

active_filters._comment: Filters currently active in the user's view that should be auto-applied (simulates page-level context)
active_filters._note: These filters are automatically applied even if not mentioned in the question!

_session_comment: Unique session ID for tracking conversation across multiple queries
_visible_tables_comment: Tables currently visible in the user's view (prioritizes these in schema context)

conversation_history.question: What is the total revenue for Italy?
How many customers do we have?

_conversation_comment: Previous questions provide context for follow-up queries
_section_example_simple_questions: ===== EXAMPLE SIMPLE QUESTIONS THAT NOW WORK =====
_examples: What is the number of customers with Complete CGR?
Show me revenue for the Italian BU
How many high-value customers do we have?
What was the churn rate last quarter?
Compare revenue between Italy and Germany
_before: Required: 'What is the num_customers within the table Initial Sizing with description Complete CGR for the BU Italy in Week 1 and where the Mandatory Version is Landline OR Mobile?'
_after: Now works: 'What is the number of customers with Complete CGR in the Italian BU in week 1?'
_reason: The tool now uses business_mappings, field_synonyms, and active_filters to understand natural language and apply implicit context automatically
